narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you're reaching for the handle. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw a 42% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 3, placing you in the lower third of your peers academically during that critical stretch, and it was the biggest signal pulling toward potential failure. Layered on top was your Year 1 decile of 5, showing your grades started middling and trended downward, painting a picture of mounting challenges rather than steady growth. Your LSAT score of 25 reinforced this, suggesting the rigorous analytical demands of law might stretch you thin compared to others who enter stronger. Coming from a low family income bracket added another layer of concern, as it often correlates with fewer resources for the intense prep the bar demands. Your Asian background, while a point of pride and resilience for many, statistically aligned with patterns the model associates with higher hurdles in this arena. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.7 couldn't fully offset these, as it took a back seat to your law school trajectory. Planning to go full-time is admirable, but the model saw it as amplifying the pressure without the buffer of part-time flexibility. Your gender didn't swing things much, but in the full mosaic, everything together suggested a riskier path to bar success. This isn't a judgment on your drive or worth—it's just the story these pieces told the model, one where the odds tipped against passing on the first try. You've got a strong UGPA foundation; imagine channeling that into boosting your deciles or LSAT prep earlier next time around. Keep pushing—you're closer than this one prediction suggests.
